pattern: Missing relationship causing wrong results
bad_dax: Sales by Category = SUM(Sales[Amount])
fixed_dax: Create relationship between Sales and Category table

pattern: Missing context transition in calculated column
bad_dax: Total = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]))
fixed_dax: Use measure instead: Total = SUM(Sales[Amount])

pattern: Ambiguous many-to-many relationship
bad_dax: Model with many-to-many between Sales and Products
fixed_dax: Introduce bridge table to resolve many-to-many relationship

pattern: Using RELATED without relationship
bad_dax: Category = RELATED(Product[Category])
fixed_dax: Create relationship between tables or use LOOKUPVALUE

pattern: Using SUMMARIZE incorrectly for aggregation
bad_dax: Table = SUMMARIZE(Sales, Sales[Region], "Total", SUM(Sales[Amount]))
fixed_dax: Use measure: Total = SUM(Sales[Amount])

pattern: Fact date used in time-intelligence function
bad_dax: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESINPERIOD(Sales[OrderDate], MAX(Sales[OrderDate]), -30, DAY))
fixed_dax: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESINPERIOD('Date'[Date], MAX('Date'[Date]), -30, DAY))

pattern: Bi-directional virtual relationship using INTERSECT
bad_dax: Matched Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], INTERSECT(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), VALUES(Sales[CustomerID])))
fixed_dax: Matched Sales = [Revenue]

pattern: Virtual relationship using CONTAINS
bad_dax: Customer Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Sales, CONTAINS(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID])))
fixed_dax: Customer Sales = [Revenue]

pattern: NATURALINNERJOIN used in measure
bad_dax: Joined Sales = SUMX(NATURALINNERJOIN(Customer, Sales), Sales[Amount])
fixed_dax: Joined Sales = SUM(Sales[Amount])

pattern: NATURALLEFTOUTERJOIN used in measure
bad_dax: Customer Revenue = SUMX(NATURALLEFTOUTERJOIN(Customer, Sales), Sales[Amount])
fixed_dax: Customer Revenue = SUM(Sales[Amount])

pattern: PATH functions used in high-cardinality measure
bad_dax: Org Depth Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Employee, PATHCONTAINS(Employee[Path], SELECTEDVALUE(Employee[EmployeeID]))))
fixed_dax: Org Depth Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], EmployeeBridge[AncestorEmployeeID] IN VALUES(Employee[EmployeeID]))

pattern: GENERATESERIES created inside measure
bad_dax: Bucket Count = COUNTROWS(FILTER(GENERATESERIES(0, MAX(Sales[Amount]), 10), [Value] <= [Revenue]))
fixed_dax: Bucket Count = COUNTROWS(FILTER(Bucket, Bucket[Value] <= [Revenue]))

pattern: INTERSECT of fact-key sets inside measure
bad_dax: Repeat Buyers = COUNTROWS(INTERSECT(VALUES(Sales[CustomerID]), VALUES(Returns[CustomerID])))
fixed_dax: Repeat Buyers = COUNTROWS(FILTER(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), [Sales Count] > 0 && [Return Count] > 0))

pattern: Measure uses bidirectional CROSSFILTER override
bad_dax: Bi Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], CROSSFILTER(Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID], BOTH))
fixed_dax: Bi Sales = [Revenue]

pattern: Flat denormalized table used instead of star schema
bad_dax: Single wide table with CustomerName, ProductName, RegionName, Date, Amount all in one table
fixed_dax: Split into: FactSales (keys + Amount), DimCustomer, DimProduct, DimRegion, DimDate. Create relationships on surrogate keys.

pattern: No dedicated Date table — using date column from fact table
bad_dax: YTD Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESYTD(Sales[OrderDate]))
fixed_dax: Create a dedicated Date table with continuous dates, mark it as Date table. Use DATESYTD('Date'[Date]) instead.

pattern: Calculated columns used for business logic instead of measures
bad_dax: Sales[Margin %] = Sales[Revenue] / Sales[Cost]
fixed_dax: Margin % = DIVIDE([Total Revenue], [Total Cost]) — use as a measure so filter context applies correctly.

pattern: Many-to-many without bridge table causes incorrect totals
bad_dax: Many-to-many relationship set between Sales[CustomerID] and Budget[CustomerID]
fixed_dax: Create a Customer bridge table with unique CustomerID. Relate Sales → Customer ← Budget through the bridge.

pattern: Role-playing date dimension not using USERELATIONSHIP
bad_dax: ShipDate Revenue = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]))
fixed_dax: ShipDate Revenue = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), USERELATIONSHIP('Date'[Date], Sales[ShipDate]))

pattern: Technical surrogate keys visible in report field list
bad_dax: CustomerKey, ProductKey, DateKey visible in field pane and draggable into visuals
fixed_dax: Right-click each key column in Model view > Hide. Users should never need to drag raw keys into visuals.

pattern: Measures not organized into display folders
bad_dax: All 40 measures in a flat unsorted list in the field pane
fixed_dax: Group measures into display folders: Revenue, Cost & Margin, Time Intelligence, KPIs, Operational. Set in Model view or Tabular Editor.

pattern: Bidirectional relationship between fact tables causes ambiguity
bad_dax: Bidirectional filter between FactSales and FactBudget
fixed_dax: Remove the bidirectional filter. Use CROSSFILTER or TREATAS inside specific measures that need cross-fact filtering.

pattern: Incremental refresh not set on large fact table
bad_dax: Full table refresh on 100M+ row fact table on every scheduled refresh
fixed_dax: Configure incremental refresh: add RangeStart/RangeEnd parameters in Power Query, set policy to store N years and refresh last N days.

pattern: Composite model DirectQuery fact table with no aggregation table
bad_dax: All visuals query DirectQuery fact table with no Import-mode aggregation layer
fixed_dax: Add an aggregation table (Import mode) summarized at day/month + key dimension granularity. Map it as user-defined aggregation in Manage Aggregations.

pattern: Snowflake schema with multi-hop dimension relationships
bad_dax: Sales → Product → Category → Division — three hops from fact to top-level dimension
fixed_dax: Flatten the dimension hierarchy into one DimProduct table with Category and Division columns. Reduces join complexity and improves query performance.

pattern: Currency conversion using SUMX without exchange rate date matching
bad_dax: USD Revenue = SUMX(Sales, Sales[Amount] * VALUES(ExchangeRate[Rate]))
fixed_dax: USD Revenue = SUMX(Sales, Sales[Amount] * LOOKUPVALUE(ExchangeRate[Rate], ExchangeRate[Date], Sales[OrderDate], ExchangeRate[Currency], Sales[Currency]))
